message:
chore(beads): close tui-upgrade Layer 0+1 beads

Closed 8 implementation beads for TUI Glamour Upgrade:
- bd-3btd6: context progress bars
- bd-ijnu3: bubbles/table pane list
- bd-gk9l7: sparkline trends
- bd-0pcdg: toast system
- bd-n3bd6: viewport scroll indicators
- bd-uz09d: spawn wizard
- bd-3xm0o: harmonica spring transitions
- bd-28vsw: gradient dividers, shimmer borders

Layer 2 QA beads (bd-jynig, bd-rpa8o) remain open.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-28n0d","title":"Implement shell environment detection","description":"Implement shell environment detection.

## What

Detect shell type and common configuration that affects agent operation.

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "shell": {
    "type": "zsh",              // bash, zsh, fish, etc.
    "tmux_plugin_detected": true,  // May cause issues
    "oh_my_zsh_detected": true,    // Common zsh framework
    "config_path": "~/.zshrc"    // Where to look for aliases
  }
}
```

## Detection Methods

### Shell Type
- Read $SHELL environment variable
- Verify against running shell process

### tmux Plugin Detection
Check common plugin locations:
- ~/.oh-my-zsh/plugins/tmux
- ~/.zshrc contains 'plugins=(... tmux ...)'
- ~/.tmux.conf sourced plugins

### oh-my-zsh Detection
- Check for ~/.oh-my-zsh directory
- Check ZSH environment variable

## Why This Matters

tmux plugins can:
- Alias the tmux command
- Add automatic session attachment
- Modify key bindings

Agents need to know to use /usr/bin/tmux directly when plugins detected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.601068157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787668899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787582285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28n0d","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.629141697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-28o9m","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing reasoning modes","description":"# Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing/browsing reasoning modes

## Command Structure
```
ntm ensemble modes [subcommand] [flags]

Subcommands:
  list    List modes (default)
  show    Show full details for a single mode
```

## List Command
```
ntm ensemble modes list [flags]
  --category string   Filter by category (e.g., Formal, Causal)
  --tier string       Filter by tier (core|advanced|experimental)
  --all               Alias for --tier all (show all tiers)
  --format string     Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose           Show full descriptions + failure modes
```

### Default Behavior
- **Default list = core tier only** (curated).
- If advanced modes exist, print a hint: `Use --all to show advanced modes`.

## Show Command
```
ntm ensemble modes show <mode-id|code> [flags]
  --format string     Output format: text, json, yaml (default: text)
```

## Behavior
- `ntm ensemble modes` with no subcommand defaults to `list`.
- Accepts lookup by **mode id** (lowercase) and **mode code** (e.g., A1).
- JSON/YAML outputs include: id, code, tier, name, category, short_desc, outputs, best_for, failure_modes, differentiator, icon, color.

## File Location
internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go

## Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog loader (bd-qn9gc)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default list shows only core tier
- [ ] Category + tier filters work
- [ ] Show displays full mode details
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs include code + tier
- [ ] `modes` defaults to `list`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:23.818764532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069282507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069258051Z","close_reason":"Implemented as 'ntm modes' command (not 'ntm ensemble modes'). Functionality complete: ntm modes list with --category, --tier, --all, --format flags; ntm modes explain for mode details. All acceptance criteria met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28o9m","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:30:47.693780272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-28v5n","title":"Task: Implement token budget advisor","description":"## Implementation
Add token estimation + budget advice:

1. Estimate per-mode cost using token heuristics + calibration data.
2. `ntm ensemble estimate` shows per-mode breakdown + total.
3. `--budget` warns if estimate exceeds limit.
4. Suggest alternative modes based on value/cost ratio.

## Logging
- Log estimates + calibration deltas + budget warnings.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation accuracy + warning thresholds
- Integration: budget warning on sample config
- E2E: estimate output + warning path

## Dependencies
- Token estimation heuristics (bd-2t4cs)
- Budget tracking (bd-1dt84, bd-3d4tl)
- Mode catalog (bd-11mtt)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:20:03.442522261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:11:26.294355492Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:11:26.294099229Z","close_reason":"Completed (already implemented; verified + tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:44:59.381622571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:06:15.515027949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-1zs9y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:20:03.471720633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-2t4cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:06:34.516791345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4tl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:06:24.275939721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-297mu","title":"Task: Add robot preflight command using DCG","description":"## Overview
Expose a robot-first command to preflight an arbitrary shell command via DCG.

## Proposed Interface
- `ntm --robot-guard --cmd "rm -rf /tmp"`
- Output: {success, allowed, reason, dcg_version}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns structured JSON with allow/deny + rationale
- Fails gracefully if dcg missing
- No command execution; preflight only","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-43oxm","title":"Unit Tests: Robot Probe","description":"Unit tests for Robot Probe command with comprehensive coverage.

## Test Coverage

### Baseline Capture Tests
- Test successful baseline capture
- Test capture with empty pane
- Test capture failure handling (session not found)
- Test content hash generation
- Test baseline comparison (changed vs unchanged)

### Flag Parsing Tests
- Test default flag values
- Test valid method flags (keystroke_echo, interrupt_test)
- Test invalid method flag error
- Test timeout range validation (100-60000)
- Test aggressive flag validation
- Test structured error output for invalid flags

### Keystroke Echo Tests
- Test responsive pane detection
- Test unresponsive pane detection
- Test latency measurement accuracy
- Test content diff detection
- Test null byte sending

### Interrupt Test Tests
- Test responsive detection
- Test interrupt behavior warning in output
- Test unresponsive detection
- Test single Ctrl-C vs double-tap distinction

### Confidence Tests
- Test high confidence: clear response, latency < 1000ms
- Test high confidence: definitely unresponsive (no activity)
- Test medium confidence: slow response
- Test medium confidence: ambiguous change
- Test low confidence: no response but process active
- Test recommendation mapping (healthy, likely_stuck, etc.)

### Timeout Tests
- Test custom timeout handling
- Test timeout expiry behavior
- Test deadline enforcement

### Edge Cases
- Test probe during pane rapid output
- Test probe during ultrathink (long processing)
- Test concurrent probes

## Logging Requirements

All tests must log:
```json
{"test": "keystroke_echo_responsive", "input": {...}, "expected": {...}, "actual": {...}, "passed": true}
```

## Mocking Strategy

- Mock tmux capture-pane output for various states
- Mock send-keys for probe input
- Simulate responsive/unresponsive scenarios
- Mock process activity checks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:33.489747284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:13:08.235424577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:13:08.235281417Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:33.518751555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-30nv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:54.048701672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-3ah0k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:57.737414902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-x0f5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:34:01.371030414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1090,7 +1090,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-mk1i5","title":"Tier 1: Safety & Reliability Integrations","description":"EPIC for Tier 1 integrations focused on immediate safety and reliability improvements.

## Why Tier 1 is Highest Priority

These three integrations address the most critical pain points in multi-agent workflows:

1. **DCG**: Prevents catastrophic mistakes. A single 'rm -rf /' or 'git push --force origin main' from any agent can destroy hours of work or worse. DCG blocks these at the command level before they execute.

2. **CAAM**: Rate limits are the #1 operational pain point. When Claude hits rate limits, you wait hours. CAAM has OAuth tokens for multiple accounts and can switch in <100ms, letting work continue immediately.

3. **caut**: Cost visibility prevents bill shock. 16+ providers tracked. Know when you're approaching limits before you hit them.

## Integration Approach

All three are Rust CLIs that work via subprocess invocation:
- DCG: Hook into PreToolUse (already exists in claude-code ecosystem)
- CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command to NTM robot mode
- caut: Add --robot-quota-status and dashboard widget

## Success Metrics

- [ ] Zero destructive commands execute across managed agents (DCG)
- [ ] Rate limit recovery time < 1 minute (CAAM)
- [ ] Quota visibility in dashboard with alerts at 80% threshold (caut)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.049682004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.558020123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.557947457Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mk1i5","depends_on_id":"bd-3fg81","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.079285837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-n5hu","title":"Task: Format and inject recovery prompt","description":"## Purpose
Synthesize all recovery context into a formatted prompt for agent injection.

## Recovery Prompt Template
# Session Recovery Context

## Your Previous Work
- You were working on: [bead title from BV]
- Last checkpoint: [timestamp] — [description]
- Files you were editing: [from Agent Mail reservations]

## Recent Messages
[Last 3 Agent Mail messages from relevant threads]

## Key Decisions Made
[From CM procedural memory for this project]

## Current Task Status
- [x] Completed: [completed beads]
- [ ] In progress: [in_progress beads]
- [ ] Blocked by: [blocking issues]

Reread AGENTS.md and continue from where you left off.

## Scope
- Format RecoveryContext into markdown prompt
- Apply token limit (config: max_recovery_tokens = 2000)
- Prioritize most relevant information if truncating
- Send to agent pane on spawn

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery prompt readable and actionable
- Respects token limit configuration
- Graceful when partial context available
- Sent before user's first prompt

## Technical Notes
Use template/text for formatting
Send via existing send mechanism","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:35:17.291725081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:05.409285011Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:05.409240778Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: FormatRecoveryPrompt function synthesizes context into markdown, token limits enforced via truncateRecoveryContext, graceful degradation tested, injection happens on spawn.  {"id":"bd-n770","title":"Add YAML swarm config file - define entire swarm (agents, prompts, assignments) in one declarative file","description":"## Overview
Define entire swarm configuration in one declarative YAML file.

## Config Format (swarm.yaml)
```yaml
name: myproject-swarm
session: myproject

agents:
  claude: 10
  codex: 10  
  gemini: 4
  
options:
  no-user: true
  auto-assign: true
  
prompts:
  init: |
    Read AGENTS.md and register with Agent Mail.
    Check your inbox and respond to any messages.
    Use bv --robot-triage to find work.
  review: |
    Review code written by fellow agents...
  post-compaction: |
    Reread AGENTS.md. Use ultrathink.

assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 24
  template: impl
  round-robin: false
  
monitor:
  stuck_threshold_seconds: 300
  auto_restart_stuck: true
  completion_patterns:
    - 'bead complete'
    - 'task finished'
```

## Commands
```bash
ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml           # Start full swarm
ntm spawn --config=swarm.yaml           # Just spawn from config
ntm assign --config=swarm.yaml          # Use assignment settings
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses YAML config format
- [ ] Validates required fields
- [ ] Supports all spawn options
- [ ] Supports prompt templates
- [ ] Supports assignment settings
- [ ] Supports monitor settings
- [ ] Clear error messages for invalid config

## Tests Required
- Unit test: YAML parsing and validation
- Unit test: Config defaults
- E2E test: Full swarm from config file","notes":"Closed as dropped: avoid new YAML format; reuse existing pipeline/recipe configs if declarative setup is needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:47.613482061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:34.562867377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:17.646542265Z","close_reason":"Dropped per canonical assign flow; redundant/overlapping entry points","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n770","depends_on_id":"bd-2rqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:19:39.439091708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-n9bcu","title":"Feature: Prompt Engine - Mode-specific preambles and injection","description":"# Feature: Prompt Engine - Mode-specific preambles with schema enforcement

Each mode agent receives a structured prompt with three mandatory components:
1. Base instructions (tone, structure, constraints)
2. Mode preamble (the reasoning 'lens')
3. **Output schema contract** (mandatory structured output)

## Prompt Structure

### 1. Base Instructions
```
You are participating in a reasoning ensemble analyzing: {problem_statement}

Context Pack:
{context_pack}

Your role: Apply your assigned reasoning mode rigorously.

Constraints:
- Stay within your reasoning mode's strengths
- Be explicit about confidence levels (numeric or high|medium|low)
- Reference specific evidence (file:line)
- Flag your mode's known failure modes
- DO NOT exceed {token_cap} tokens
```

### 2. Mode Preamble (The Lens)
```
## Your Reasoning Mode: {mode_name}

{mode_description}

What you excel at:
{best_for}

How you differ from other modes:
{differentiator}

Common failure modes to avoid:
{failure_modes}
```

### 3. Output Schema Contract (MANDATORY)
```
## REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT

You MUST structure your response as valid YAML with these exact fields:

mode_id: {mode_id}           # optional; system will inject if missing
thesis: |
  Your one-sentence main insight
confidence: 0.0-1.0           # numeric or high|medium|low

top_findings:
  - finding: Description of finding
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    confidence: 0.0-1.0        # numeric or high|medium|low
    evidence_pointer: path/to/file.go:142
    reasoning: How you reached this

risks:
  - risk: Description of risk
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    likelihood: 0.0-1.0        # numeric or high|medium|low
    mitigation: Suggested mitigation
    affected_areas: [string]

recommendations:
  - recommendation: What to do
    priority: critical|high|medium|low
    effort: low|medium|high
    rationale: Why this helps
    related_findings: [int]    # indices into top_findings (0-based)

questions_for_user:
  - question: What needs clarification?
    context: Why this matters
    blocking: false
    suggested_answers: [string]

failure_modes_to_watch:
  - mode: Potential failure of my analysis
    description: What could go wrong
    indicators: [string]
    prevention: How to guard against it

NON-COMPLIANT OUTPUTS WILL BE REJECTED.
```

## Schema Validation

PromptInjector validates all outputs:
- Parse YAML structure
- Check required fields present
- Validate enum values (impact/priority/etc.)
- Accept numeric or qualitative likelihood/confidence values
- Ensure evidence_pointers are valid file references
- Validate list fields when present (affected_areas, related_findings, suggested_answers, indicators)
- Inject mode_id if missing (warn)
- Flag non-compliant outputs for human review

## Testing Coverage
- Unit: bd-xkrnc (preamble rendering + schema contract fields)
- Unit: bd-qeoex (schema validator exhaustive cases)
- E2E: bd-18ds7 (full pipeline includes preamble injection + schema validation)

## Files
- internal/ensemble/preamble.go - Template structure
- internal/ensemble/preambles/ - Mode-specific content
- internal/ensemble/schema_validator.go - Output validation
- internal/swarm/prompt_injector.go - Injection logic

## Logging Requirements
- Log prompt assembly start with mode_id, mode_code, pane, agent_type, token_cap
- Log schema contract version/hash and missing required fields
- Log validation summary (valid/invalid counts, first error per mode)
- Log injection outcome per pane (success/failure + error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompt assembly deterministic for same inputs (stable ordering)
- [ ] Output schema contract matches OutputSchema types (no drift)
- [ ] Schema validation rejects non-compliant outputs and reports errors
- [ ] Logging captures per-mode injection and validation status","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:25:44.569622243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:44:13.143101802Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:44:13.142546325Z","close_reason":"Feature complete - Prompt Engine with mode-specific preambles and schema enforcement:

## Implementation Summary
All components verified complete:
1. **preamble.go** - PreambleEngine with base instructions template and schema contract
2. **injector.go** - EnsembleInjector wrapping BasicInjector for mode-aware injection
3. **schema_validator.go** - Full validation of ModeOutput with ParseYAML/Normalize/Validate
4. **types.go** - OutputSchema types (ModeOutput, Finding, Risk, Recommendation, Question, FailureModeWarning)

## Acceptance Criteria Verified
- [x] Prompt assembly deterministic (Go templates with stable ordering)
- [x] Schema contract matches OutputSchema types (all fields aligned)
- [x] Schema validation rejects non-compliant outputs with detailed errors
- [x] Logging captures mode_id, mode_code, pane, agent_type, success/failure

## Key Features
- Three-section preamble: base instructions + mode section + schema contract
- Context pack rendering with project brief and user context
- Mode metadata auto-generation from ReasoningMode fields
- SchemaVersion tracking (1.0.0)
- Support for numeric or qualitative confidence/likelihood values","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-1hlhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:25:23.693018286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:25:14.369977867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-2pl9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:25:32.536294613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T18:00:49.693486889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-4io74","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:25:41.175605558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-qeoex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:25:57.799464811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-wx2qv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:30:02.315932927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-xkrnc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:25:49.668123883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ughtn","title":"Feature: Mode Explanation Cards","description":"## Summary
Provide explanation cards for each reasoning mode: what it does, when to use it, and example outputs.

## Rationale
80 modes is overwhelming. Cards reduce trial-and-error and help users build better ensembles.

## Implementation
- ModeCard data lives alongside the mode catalog (validated, deterministic).
- `ntm modes explain <mode>` renders a card (human + JSON).
- Dashboard/TUI shows cards on hover/click.
- Dry-run preview links to card summaries.

## Logging
- Log explain command usage + mode_id

## Tests
- Unit: every mode has a complete card
- Unit: JSON schema validation for cards
- E2E: CLI explain output formatting

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every mode has a complete explanation card
- [ ] CLI explain command works (human + JSON)
- [ ] Dashboard shows interactive cards
- [ ] Cards include usage guidance + examples

## Dependencies
- Requires embedded mode catalog (bd-11mtt)
- Requires mode metadata extensions (bd-3806s)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:07:25.577850463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T02:48:56.593539002Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T02:48:56.593381265Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ughtn","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:58:55.146330286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ughtn","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:11:19.771804082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ughtn","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:59:10.069779862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-uptv","title":"E2E Tests: Round-robin distribution with multiple agent types","description":"## Test Coverage
E2E test of round-robin strategy via `ntm assign --strategy=round-robin` across mixed agent types.

## Test Scenario
1. Spawn mixed agents: --cc=2 --cod=2 --gmi=1
2. Create 10 test beads
3. Run: `ntm assign <session> --strategy=round-robin --limit=10`
4. Verify even distribution

## Expected Distribution (10 beads / 5 agents)
- Claude 1: beads 1, 6
- Claude 2: beads 2, 7
- Codex 1: beads 3, 8
- Codex 2: beads 4, 9
- Gemini 1: beads 5, 10

## Verification
- Each agent gets exactly 2 beads
- Beads assigned in order
- No duplicates

## Logging Requirements
```bash
log_e2e() {
    echo "[E2E-ROUNDROBIN] $(date -Iseconds) test=$1 step=$2 status=$3 details=\"$4\""
}

# Example usage:
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "spawn_agents" "start" "cc=2 cod=2 gmi=1"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "spawn_agents" "complete" "panes=5"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "create_beads" "complete" "count=10"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "assign" "start" "strategy=round-robin limit=10"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "assign" "complete" "assigned=10 agents=5"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "verify" "checking" "agent=cc_1 expected=2"
log_e2e "round_robin_distribute" "verify" "pass" "all agents got exactly 2 beads"
```

## Test File
tests/e2e/test_round_robin_assign.sh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All agents receive equal number of beads
- [ ] Assignment order is deterministic
- [ ] Robot output JSON is valid
- [ ] Detailed logging at each step
- [ ] Test passes in CI environment","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:22:00.649949242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:35.128774975Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:35.128728166Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uptv","depends_on_id":"bd-l4y4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:22:55.332334457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-uptv","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:40.114566347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-uzasi","title":"Task: Implement mode output caching system","description":"## Implementation
Add caching layer for mode outputs (prefer existing internal/ensemble files; add new file only if needed):

1. CacheManager:
   - Disk-backed store under `.ntm/ensemble-cache/`
   - `CacheKey = ContextHash + ModeID + ModeVersion + ConfigHash`
   - Methods: Get/Put/Invalidate/Stats

2. Invalidation:
   - Context hash change
   - Mode version bump
   - TTL expiry (default 24h, configurable)
   - `--no-cache` explicit bypass

3. CLI integration:
   - `--use-cache/--no-cache`
   - `ntm ensemble cache stats` and `cache clear`

## Logging
- Log hit/miss + invalidation reason per mode

## Tests
- Unit: key derivation + TTL expiry
- Integration: cache persists across runs
- E2E: rerun uses cache; `--no-cache` forces refresh

## Dependencies
- Requires ModeOutput schema (bd-1wnxl)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:40:30.293022655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T08:45:20.680447825Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:45:20.680374118Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uzasi","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-uzasi","depends_on_id":"bd-3uydb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
